@graph.description:
We want to recreate the ENCODE H3K4me3 ChIP-seq experiment in K562 cells (ENCODE accession ENCSR000EWA). The experiment has two biological replicates of 36-bp single-end Illumina HiSeq 2000 reads with a matched input chromatin control. H3K4me3 is a sharp promoter-associated histone mark, so we want narrow-peak calling (not the broad-peak shape used for repressive marks). Reference genome is GRCh38 with the ENCODE pipeline reference TSV.

Follow the ENCODE chip-seq-pipeline2 (github.com/ENCODE-DCC/chip-seq-pipeline2) histone-narrow conventions: FastQC for per-file QC, BWA aln + samse for alignment with ENCODE parameter set (-q 5 -l 32 -k 2) or Bowtie2 with default histone settings, Picard MarkDuplicates with duplicates removed, MAPQ >= 30 filter, ENCODE blacklist v2 (ENCFF356LFX) subtracted, phantompeakqualtools (run_spp.R) for cross-correlation NSC / RSC, MACS2 callpeak in narrow mode (-f BAM -g hs --nomodel --extsize <spp-derived-fraglen> -p 0.01 --keep-dup all --call-summits) per replicate against matched input, then IDR with --rank signal.value --soft-idr-threshold 0.05 on rep1-vs-rep2 plus pseudo-replicate IDR for self-consistency.

Outputs: per-replicate narrow-peak BED files; IDR-replicated consensus peak BED; BigWig genome tracks per replicate (RPGC-normalized); standard ENCODE QC table: usable read count per replicate, NSC, RSC, NRF, PBC1, PBC2, FRiP. Annotate the consensus peak set against RefSeq TSS coordinates (HOMER annotatePeaks.pl or ChIPseeker): expected enrichment is heavily at promoters (within +/- 2 kb of any RefSeq TSS) for >= 90% of peaks. Compare the recreated IDR consensus set against the ENCODE-published default-analysis IDR peak file for ENCSR000EWA via bedtools jaccard.

For motif sanity-check, run MEME-ChIP or HOMER findMotifsGenome.pl on the top 500 peaks by signalValue; H3K4me3 promoter peaks should not show a strong sequence-specific TF motif as the top de novo motif (in contrast to TF ChIP-seq); accept any GC-rich CpG-island-like motif as the top hit.

Acceptance: consensus IDR-replicated peak count within +/-15% of ENCODE's released peak count for ENCSR000EWA; Jaccard overlap with ENCODE published IDR peak set >= 0.90; promoter overlap >= 90% of called peaks; NSC >= 1.05; RSC >= 0.8; FRiP >= 0.05; NRF >= 0.9.
@graph.name:
computational biology — ChIP-seq peak calling + peak-vs-input enrichment. Standard pipeline:
raw QC, trim, align, call peaks against the matched input control, then
report. Mirrors today's `config/stage-taxonomies/chip-seq.yaml`.

@graph.description: The domain expert who resolved discovery decisions during intake chat, prior to agent execution.
@graph.name: Subject Matter Expert (intake)
Agent Instructions
Workflow Context
Discovery and scoring policies
Execution provenance log
Homo sapiens

Retrieve prior literature (PMC OA full-text XML by default, broader via
SWFC_LIT_SOURCE_SCOPE) on the analysis's target entities + tissue +
modality. Emit a structured prior_claims_matrix.csv where every row is
PMID-anchored with a substring-verified evidence_quote. No biological
synthesis, no narrative review.

Validate outputs of: Retrieve prior literature (PMC OA full-text XML by default, broader via
SWFC_LIT_SOURCE_SCOPE) on the analysis's target entities + tissue +
modality. Emit a structured prior_claims_matrix.csv where every row is
PMID-anchored with a substring-verified evidence_quote. No biological
synthesis, no narrative review.
Discover method for sequence_trimming (options: trimmomatic, cutadapt, fastp, trim_galore). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for peak_calling (options: macs2, macs3, seacr, genrich). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for alignment (options: star, hisat2, bwa_mem2, bwa_mem, minimap2, bowtie2). Surface set_intake_method.

Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Validate outputs of: Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Validate outputs of: Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Validate outputs of: Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Validate outputs of: Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Identify regions of significant read enrichment from ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq /
CUT&RUN alignments. MACS2 / MACS3 / SEACR / Genrich selected at runtime
by mark type (narrow / broad).

Validate outputs of: Identify regions of significant read enrichment from ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq /
CUT&RUN alignments. MACS2 / MACS3 / SEACR / Genrich selected at runtime
by mark type (narrow / broad).

Map each row of the upstream findings table (DE genes / called peaks /
called variants) to PMID-anchored prior-work rows. Emit
claims_evidence_matrix.csv with deterministic concordance flags
(same_direction / opposite_direction / no_prior_finding / unverifiable).
No synthesis; no narrative.

Validate outputs of: Map each row of the upstream findings table (DE genes / called peaks /
called variants) to PMID-anchored prior-work rows. Emit
claims_evidence_matrix.csv with deterministic concordance flags
(same_direction / opposite_direction / no_prior_finding / unverifiable).
No synthesis; no narrative.

Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Validate outputs of: Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.

Validate outputs of: Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.
